chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-2028 of 2024 Date Order with signature of Judge FRESH CASE. 1. For orders on Misc. No.9179 of 2024 2. For orders on office objection No.20 and 27. 3. For orders on Misc. No.9180 of 2024 4. For hearing of main case. 30-04-2024 M/s. Zubair Ali Khaskheli and Rustam Khan Mahar, Advocates for Petitioner. --- 1. Granted. 2-4. After hearing learned counsel for the petitioner at some length, it appears that the Petitioner having history of litigation with private respondents, has expressed personal grievance against blockage of un- metaled road going towards main roads of the village by the private parties allegedly with the connivance of Area Police and District Administration of Thatta. However, the learned counsel for Petitioner has not been able to point out any material showing the blockage of road, thoroughfare by the Respondents. It has been submitted that some Huts have been established by the private Respondents on public land/road causing blockage of way. According to learned counsel for the Petitioner since the Deputy Commissioner [Thatta] and the Area Police have not taken any action against the private respondents, therefore, requests that directions may be issued to official respondents that legal action may be taken against private respondents. It appears that some dispute with regard to encroachment over the land has arisen between the private parties, which cannot be resolved through Constitutional Petition, as it may require evidence with regard to title possession etc. In view of the above, instant Petition stands dismissed alongwith listed application[s].

It appears that some dispute with regard to encroachment over the land has arisen between the private parties, which cannot be resolved through Constitutional Petition, as it may require evidence with regard to title possession etc. In view of the above, instant Petition stands dismissed alongwith listed application[s]. However, Petitioner is at liberty to approach the concerned authorities, including the Deputy Commissioner [Thatta], SSP/SHO, Thatta, or the Anti-Encroachment Department, which reporting the illegal blockage of road, public thoroughfare, or encroachment, if any, by the private respondents, who shall take appropriate action in accordance with the law. CHIEF JUSTICE J U D G E Jamil
